betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Heat Sink§f:
A Fission Heat Sink is a passive cooling component placed inside a Solid Fission Reactor. It absorbs heat from adjacent fuel cells, allowing the reactor to operate at sustainable temperatures.

§6Crafting Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:277-301)§f:
The base Empty Heat Sink is created via addShapelessFluidRecipe using a Bucket of Water. Each material variant then upgrades the Empty Heat Sink:
- §bIron Heat Sink§f (meta 1): "I I / ISI / I I" — Iron surrounding Empty Heat Sink
- §bRedstone Heat Sink§f (meta 2): "RRR / RSR / RRR" — Redstone surrounding Empty Heat Sink
- §bQuartz Heat Sink§f (meta 3): "QQQ / QSQ / QQQ" — Quartz surrounding Empty Heat Sink
- §bObsidian Heat Sink§f (meta 4): "DOD / OSO / DOD" — Obsidian + Obsidian Dust
- §bNether Brick Heat Sink§f (meta 5): "INI / NSN / INI" — Nether Brick + Ingot BrickNether
- §bGlowstone Heat Sink§f (meta 6): "GGG / GSG / GGG" — Glowstone Dust
- §bLapis Heat Sink§f (meta 7): "LLL / LSL / LLL" — Lapis Lazuli (the task item for this quest)
- §bGold Heat Sink§f (meta 8): " G / GSG / G " — Gold Nugget
- §bPrismarine Heat Sink§f (meta 9): " P / PSP / P " — Prismarine
- §bSlime Heat Sink§f (meta 10): "BBB / BSB / BBB" — Slimeball
- §bEnd Stone Heat Sink§f (meta 11): "DED / ESE / DED" — End Stone + End Stone Dust
- §bPurpur Heat Sink§f (meta 12): "CPC / PSP / CPC" — Purpur Block + Chorus Fruit Popped
- §bDiamond Heat Sink§f (meta 13): " D / DSD / D " — Diamond
- §bEmerald Heat Sink§f (meta 14): " E / ESE / E " — Emerald
- §bCopper Heat Sink§f (meta 15): " C / CSC / C " — Copper Ingot

§6Tier-2 Heat Sinks (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:293-301)§f:
NC's solid_fission_sink2 has additional high-tier variants (Tin, Lead, Boron, Lithium, Magnesium, Manganese, Aluminum, Silver, Fluorite). These are mid-game upgrades with better cooling rates.

§6What Heat Sinks Do§f:
Each heat sink placed inside the reactor:
- Absorbs heat from adjacent fuel cells (rate depends on material)
- Has a fixed heat capacity (overfilling causes it to vaporize)
- Acts as a passive cooler (no fluid input needed)
- Can be replaced while the reactor is running (no shutdown required)

§6Cooling Material Tier List (effective cooling rate)§f:
Low tier: Water, Iron, Tin
Mid tier: Redstone, Copper, Lapis, Lead
High tier: Quartz, Glowstone, Boron, Lithium
Top tier: Diamond, Emerald, Magnesium Diboride (separate component)

§6Direct Descendants of Lapis Heat Sink (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The task items are:
- nuclearcraft:solid_fission_sink:7 (Lapis Heat Sink)
- nuclearcraft:solid_fission_sink:1 (Iron Heat Sink) — the basic alternative

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30044 (Inside the Reactor) — the interior shell.

§eTip§f: For early-game, Iron Heat Sinks are cheap and sufficient for a small 5×5×5 LEU reactor. Scale up to Lapis or Redstone sinks as you transition to HEU fuels.

§eTip§f: Combine Heat Sinks with Cell Ports to extract absorbed heat as fluid (water -> steam, NaK -> hot NaK, helium -> hot helium). This fluid can drive a Turbine for additional power.

§cWarning§f: Heat Sinks have a maximum heat capacity. If the reactor is producing heat faster than the sinks can absorb it, the sinks will vaporize (destroyed) and the reactor will start overheating. Monitor heat with a Thermometer or Reactor Info Panel.
